page: Amazon Web Services a1.4xlarge is an ARM-based virtual machine instance powered by the first-generation AWS Graviton processor. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance features 16 dedicated physical cores utilizing the ARMv8 Cortex-A72 architecture running at 2.3 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core allocation. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate moderate capabilities, with average results in multi-core processing and software compilation. The stress-ng multi-core CPU benchmark yields 23858.27, while the single-core score is 1491.48. Memory bandwidth shows a significant variance, delivering an average 566.18 GB/sec for small cached blocks, but dropping to a weak 32.43 GB/sec for large uncached blocks and a poor 10.01 GB/sec for large writes. Single-threaded tasks, such as gzip compression and text processing, perform poorly, whereas multi-threaded operations like bzip3 decompression and database operations achieve average performance tiers.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the a1.4xlarge offers an entry-level ARM alternative for workloads that benefit from physical core dedication without requiring high single-thread performance or massive memory footprints. The lack of local storage and lower memory-to-CPU ratio represent key tradeoffs. This instance is best suited for scale-out workloads, multi-threaded applications, software compilation, and basic database operations that can leverage the ARM64 architecture efficiently.
description: Amazon Web Services a1.4xlarge is an ARM-based instance powered by a first-generation AWS Graviton processor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores utilizing the ARMv8 Cortex-A72 architecture running at 2.3 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory. The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor, provides a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps, and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show average multi-core CPU performance and software compilation capabilities, alongside efficient multi-threaded decompression. However, single-threaded tasks and uncached memory write speeds are weak. This instance offers a qualitatively cost-effective profile for scale-out workloads, ARM64 software development, multi-threaded applications, and basic database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services a1.4xlarge is an ARM64 instance featuring 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 32 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for cost-effective scale-out and multi-threaded workloads.

tagline: An ARM-powered dedicated core instance designed for cost-efficient scale-out workloads, multi-threaded applications, and software compilation.
